title: festuca rubra subsp. multiflora
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. multiflora, also known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties. Festuca rubra subsp. multiflora has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
